methods.method_name: Pollen Season Definition and SPI Calculation
methods.description: This method defines the pollen season based on daily mean pollen concentration and calculates the Seasonal Pollen Index (SPI) as a total of daily average pollen counts in a given season.

steps.description: The pollen season in particular years was defined as the period from which the sum of the daily mean pollen concentration reached 2.5% of the total sum until the time when the sum reached 97.5%, i.e., the time with 95% of the whole pollen count (95% method).
steps.searchable_summary: define pollen season based on daily mean pollen concentration
outputs: pollen season start and end dates

steps.description: The SPI was calculated as a total of daily average pollen counts in a given season.

methods.description: Statistical procedures used to analyze the collected pollen data, including normality tests, calculation of descriptive statistics, and correlation analysis.

steps.description: The distributions of the data were not normal (Shapiro-Wilk test) and statistical error risk was estimated at a significance level of P < 0.05, 0.01, and 0.001.
steps.searchable_summary: perform Shapiro-Wilk test for normality and estimate statistical error risk

steps.description: Mean, minimum and maximum values, standard deviation, variation coefficient, skewness, and kurtosis were calculated.
The linear trend in the determination coefficient (R 2 ) was calculated using Excel (MS Office 2010).
steps.searchable_summary: calculate linear trend and determination coefficient

steps.description: Spearman's correlations between the linear trend and selected features of the pollen season were calculated using Statistica ver.11.
The degree of correlation between particular meteorological parameters, air pollution and the concentration of Betula in Szczecin was analyzed by non-parametric Spearman's correlation test.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze correlation between meteorological parameters, air pollution, and Betula concentration

methods.method_name: Trend Analysis of Pollen Season Features
methods.description: Trend analysis to illustrate and predict trends of selected features of birch pollen seasons, including start and end dates, duration, and maximum values.

steps.description: Trend lines were used to illustrate the trends of selected features of birch pollen seasons and to analyze the issues related to prediction.
steps.searchable_summary: create trend lines for pollen season features

steps.description: During the fourteen-year study period, weak but statistically insignificant trends in the start of the Betula pollen season were observed.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze trends in pollen season start dates

steps.description: Weak and statistically significant trends for the ends of pollen seasons were found in certain cities.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze trends in pollen season end dates

steps.description: A weak increasing trend in pollen season duration was observed in some cities.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze trends in pollen season duration

steps.description: A strong statistically insignificant trend towards a decrease in the maximum value was found for some cities.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze trends in pollen season maximum values

steps.description: Most often, statistically significant trends were observed for the date of the maximum value.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze trends in dates of maximum pollen values
